mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts directly increase corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show negative mean when RSAFS and HOUST both increase month-over-month, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly cut in the federal funds rate immediately lowers short-term discount rates, triggering algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns within the next trading week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by > 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in > 55% of instances following a > 0.25% rate cut, or event study alpha is statistically insignificant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust labor market tightening, which compresses equity risk premiums and generates positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the index of industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing output, which reduces corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the predictive slope coefficient turns positive during late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces central banks to tighten policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL is followed by positive 20-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the correlation between CPI growth and forward returns exceeds +0.10 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth exceeding 3.0% confirms robust aggregate demand, prompting institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: GDP releases above 3.0% are followed by negative 5-day returns in > 60% of instances, or the event window alpha fails to reach statistical significance at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling unemployment and rising industrial production indicates a healthy economic expansion where labor absorption outpaces output constraints, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shocks in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated housing starts combined with rising real consumption expenditures signal strong household balance sheets, which sustains consumer spending and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: High housing and consumption levels coincide with negative 20-day returns in > 55% of observations, or the predictive power disappears when inflation exceeds 3.0% annually.
